summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates CPU exhaustion through catastrophic regex backtracking. CoreDNS processes malicious regex patterns without complexity validation, allowing DoS attacks.
expected_security_impact: When CoreDNS processes a regex pattern with nested quantifiers (.*.*)*$ and receives a crafted DNS query, catastrophic backtracking should cause CPU exhaustion and slow or fail DNS responses.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built CoreDNS with a malicious Corefile containing ReDoS-prone regex. Crafted DNS queries triggered high CPU usage through regex backtracking, demonstrating the DoS vulnerability.
verification_method: The verification script monitors container CPU usage and DNS response times. High CPU usage (>50%) or slow DNS responses (>2000ms) indicate successful DoS through regex backtracking.
evidence_produced_by_run: CoreDNS compiled regex pattern (.*.*)*$ without complexity validation
Crafted DNS queries triggered regex matching with catastrophic backtracking
Elevated CPU usage detected during query processing
DNS responses delayed or failed due to CPU exhaustion
No timeout or resource limits on regex matching
limitations_or_blockers: ReDoS effectiveness varies based on regex pattern and query string
May require multiple concurrent queries for maximum impact
Modern Go versions have some regex optimizations that may reduce impact

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the CoreDNS repository from GitHub, checks out v1.11.1, and builds from source
